en: I guess you had to stop off at the Asian fighter plane store.
ko: 아시아 전투기 가게에 들러야 했던 것 같군요.

en: No, actually, sir, that's a surveillance drone
ko: 아니요, 사실 선생님, 그건 감시 드론이에요.

en: with outstanding solar cells.
It's Indian.
Take a seat.
So we got Tom's scores back.
He's going to make an excellent farmer.
Yeah, he's got a knack for it.
What about college?
Well, the university only takes a handful.
They don't really have the resources.
They still pay my taxes.
ko: 그래도 제 세금은 내요. 그 돈은 어디로 가죠?

en: Now, where does that money go? There's no more armies.
Well, it doesn't go to the university.
Look, Coop, you have to be realistic.
You're ruling my son out for college now? The kid's 15.
ko: 지금 제 아들을 대학에서 제외하겠다는 건가요? 그 아이는 15살이에요.

en: Tom's score simply isn't high enough.
ko: 톰의 성적이 충분히 높지 않아요. 당신 허리 사이즈는요?

en: What's your waistline? About what, 32?
I'm about 33, NC. I'm not sure I see what you're getting at.
ko: 저는 33이에요, NC. 당신이 무슨 말을 하고 싶은지 잘 모르겠어요.

en: Are you telling me it takes two numbers to measure your own ass,
ko: 자신의 엉덩이를 재는 데 두 숫자가 필요한데,

en: but only one to measure my son's future? Come on.
ko: 제 아들의 미래를 재는 데는 하나만 필요하다는 건가요? 제발.

en: You're a well-educated man, Coop, and a trained pilot. And an engineer.
ko: 당신은 잘 교육받은 사람이고, 훈련된 조종사이며, 엔지니어예요.
